title: `podman kube play` - automatic cold/hot re-load/re-build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've been evaluating various tools such as Podman, Gitpod, Codespaces, Skaffold, Draft, DevSpace, Telepresence, Docker Dev Environments, Coder. 

It's a busy space right now! (But who will win eh?)

**Describe the results you received:**

Comparables have a feature where you can edit source code and the container is re-built. E.g.:

* Auto-rebuild on source change.
* Rsync or mirror source code.
* Hot-reload.

comments.body: How would you envision this applying to podman kube play? Why wouldn't a higher level tool just watch the kube.yaml file and rerun podman kube play --replace?
That would work. I think it would depend on the scope of `kube play`. IHMO making it a more full feature dev tool is probably not what the authors envisaged, yet without this if feels … incomplete.

I think in another PR you talked about a --watch option, in addition to a --wait option.
Is this what you envision, where we would create pods based on a k8s yaml file, and then have podman monitor the file for changes (potentially via fnotify) and then if the YAML file changes, recreate the pods.

No. These are two different features. `--watch` should display the correct state of the pod, similar to `Kubectl describe pod`. Auto-build should monitor the build directory (e.. `image`) and if that changes, re-build.

The challenge is - that directory probably does not contain everything need for the build.
Would `podman kube describe YAML` make sense for that command?
I guess I want that to automatically update. Just like `docker compose up`

When you say `docker compose up` automatically updates, you are saying that a change to a Dockerfile or the YAML will cause the image to be rebuilt and containers recreated?

Or does the user have to run another `docker compose up` command to trigger this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
